findings.message: New dependency 'requests' is added without a version constraint, while every other dependency in this manifest uses a lower-bound pin (e.g. '>=...'). An unpinned entry makes the resolved version non-deterministic across environments and weakens supply-chain reproducibility.
findings.suggestion: Pin a minimum version consistent with the rest of the manifest, e.g. 'requests>=2.32'.

findings.message: 'requests' duplicates capability already provided by an existing dependency. The project is async (FastAPI/uvicorn) and ships 'httpx>=0.28' in the dev group; httpx offers a fully-featured sync AND async HTTP client, so adding 'requests' introduces a second HTTP stack (extra transitive deps like urllib3/charset-normalizer, extra audit surface) with no capability gain. 'requests' is also in maintenance-only mode upstream, whereas httpx is actively developed.
findings.suggestion: Drop the 'requests' addition and use httpx for HTTP calls (promoting it from the dev group to runtime dependencies if it is needed at runtime); this keeps a single HTTP client across sync/async paths.
